panels.description: Wide shot, cold, eerie space atmosphere. A silhouette of a Martian creature floating in a dark chamber, indifferent and alien. Cinematic lighting with deep shadows.

bubbles.text: The Martians were lifted above all organic fluctuations of mood and emotion. Their need for us as nourishment was merely a necessity of their biology.

panels.description: Medium shot of a Martian corpse specimen displayed in a laboratory setting. Pale, flimsy silicious skeleton, six feet tall, round head with large flinty eyes. Harsh white medical lighting.

bubbles.text: Flimsy skeletons, feeble musculature. Earth's gravity alone would have shattered them.

panels.description: Close-up on a notebook or desk where the author is writing. Focus on the anatomical diagrams of the Martian nervous system. The atmosphere is academic and investigative.

bubbles.text: Their physiology differed strangely. They had no need for sleep; their hearts never rested, for they had no fatigue to overcome.

panels.description: Wide, cinematic shot of multiple Martians working in a synchronized, endless loop in a sterile metallic environment. They appear as mindless, efficient machines.

bubbles.text: Twenty-four hours of constant labor. Absolutely without sex, and therefore, without any of the tumultuous emotions that define humanity.

panels.description: Detail shot: a small Martian budding off a parent, looking like a biological growth. In the background, a portrait of a Victorian scientist looking concerned.

bubbles.text: It is a chilling mirror. A speculative writer once foresaw our own human civilization evolving toward this same soulless end.
